description_long: This object consists of two adjacent wooden shelving units, each with a rectangular frame and a flat top surface. The left unit features a black, grid-patterned section on its top, possibly a vented or slatted surface, while the right unit has a plain wooden top. Both units have a back panel and a lower shelf, with a small circular hole in the center of the main shelf, likely for cable management. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the object a natural, warm appearance. The structure is boxy and robust, suitable for use as a storage or display unit in urban environments such as parks, bus stops, or public waiting areas. The overall design is functional, with clean lines and a utilitarian aesthetic.
description: A pair of wooden shelving units, one with a black grid-patterned top and the other with a plain wooden surface, both featuring back panels and lower shelves.
description_view_0: Front view showing both shelving units, highlighting the black grid-patterned top on the left unit and the plain wooden top on the right.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the units and the layered construction of the back panels.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full extent of the grid pattern on the left unit and the open surfaces of both units.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid wooden sides and the relative positioning of the two units.
